methods.description: Measuring cell density of diatom cultures using a Coulter counter and a Neubauer haemocytometer.

steps.description: Periodic subsamples were collected for cell density determination...
steps.searchable_summary: Collect periodic subsamples from culture

steps.description: ...using a Coulter counter (Beckman Coulter Inc., Brea, CA, USA)...
steps.searchable_summary: Determine cell density using Coulter counter

steps.description: ...and a Neubauer haemocytometer.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine cell density using Neubauer haemocytometer

methods.description: Culturing diatoms under controlled temperature and light conditions using a specific growth medium, followed by harvesting biomass at the stationary phase and initial storage.

steps.description: Each batch was cultured at 15°C...
steps.searchable_summary: Culture diatoms at 15°C

steps.description: ...illuminated with a photon flux of 200 μmol/m 2 /s (approx. 43.6 W/m 2 ) using in-built fluorescent tubing on an 18:6 h day:night cycle.
steps.searchable_summary: Provide illumination (200 μmol/m2/s) on 18:6 h light:dark cycle

steps.description: For growth medium, the CCAP recipe 'Diatom Medium' (DM) was used, 37 buffered to pH 7 using tris(hydroxymelthyl)aminomethane (TRIS).
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare CCAP 'Diatom Medium' buffered to pH 7 with TRIS

steps.description: Each sample represented an amalgamation of 80 L of culture, spread over eight 10-L polycarbonate carboys and two cabinets.
steps.searchable_summary: Scale up culture to 80 L in 10-L carboys

steps.description: Each carboy was aerated by bubbling sterile filtered air, which ensured perpetual cell suspension within the culture.
steps.searchable_summary: Aerate carboys by bubbling sterile filtered air

steps.description: Cells were harvested by centrifugation at the onset of the stationary growth phase in order to maximise harvested biomass.
steps.searchable_summary: Harvest cells by centrifugation at stationary phase

steps.description: Following centrifugation, the organic diatom pellet was frozen at −80°C...
steps.searchable_summary: Freeze cell pellet at -80°C

steps.description: ...and freeze-dried for storage.
steps.searchable_summary: Freeze-dry the frozen pellet for storage

methods.description: Removing organic matter from harvested and freeze-dried diatom biomass using a sequential chemical digestion procedure involving H2O2, aqua regia, and HCl.

steps.description: Removal of organic matter was undertaken using a sequential digestion using H 2 O 2 ...
steps.searchable_summary: Perform digestion step using H2O2

steps.description: ...HNO 3 + HCl (aqua regia; 1:3 ratio)...
steps.searchable_summary: Perform digestion step using aqua regia (HNO3 + HCl 1:3)

steps.description: ...and conc. HCl...
steps.searchable_summary: Perform digestion step using concentrated HCl

steps.description: ...at 60°C in a heated water bath...
steps.searchable_summary: Maintain temperature at 60°C during digestion

steps.description: ...with repeated rinsing in 10 MΩ deionised water between phases.
steps.searchable_summary: Rinse repeatedly with 10 MΩ deionised water between digestion steps

steps.description: Due to the amount and resistance of the organic-rich samples, the digestion procedure took 1 week to reach completion.
steps.searchable_summary: Allow digestion to proceed for up to 1 week until completion

steps.description: The final mass of silica per sample was of the order of 0.5 g.
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain cleaned silica sample (approx. 0.5 g)

methods.description: Setting up and running experiments to assess oxygen isotopic exchange between fresh diatoms and water with different isotopic compositions by incubating samples at elevated temperature.

steps.description: In order to assess the oxygen isotopic exchange between fresh diatoms and water, two waters of different δ 18 O values...
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare two water sources with different δ18O values

steps.description: ...incubated fresh diatoms in these waters at 80°C for 1 week...
steps.searchable_summary: Incubate fresh diatoms in prepared waters at 80°C for 1 week

steps.description: ...after which time the silica was concentrated by centrifugation...
steps.searchable_summary: Concentrate silica from incubation mixture by centrifugation

steps.description: ...and the supernatant water was decanted...
steps.searchable_summary: Decant supernatant water

steps.description: ...before the sample was transferred to a 2-mL centrifuge tube.
steps.searchable_summary: Transfer silica sample to 2-mL centrifuge tube

steps.description: The silica samples were then frozen (4°C)...
steps.searchable_summary: Freeze silica samples at 4°C

steps.description: ...and freeze-dried.
steps.searchable_summary: Freeze-dry the frozen silica samples

methods.method_name: Water Oxygen Isotope Monitoring and Analysis
methods.description: Monitoring and analyzing the oxygen isotope composition of water samples collected before and after the isotope exchange experiments using the equilibration method and mass spectrometry.

steps.description: A subsample of the initial water source was set aside ('start water', kept constant for each aliquot)...
steps.searchable_summary: Collect and set aside subsample of initial water ('start water')

steps.description: ...and 5-mL samples of supernatant water were collected at the end of each experiment ('end water').
steps.searchable_summary: Collect 5-mL samples of supernatant water at the end of each experiment ('end water')

steps.description: The water samples were stored refrigerated in air-tight HDPE bottles with minimal headspace...
steps.searchable_summary: Store water samples refrigerated in air-tight HDPE bottles

steps.description: ...and analysed within 1 month of collection.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze water samples within 1 month of collection

steps.description: In some circumstances during storage at 80°C, the pressure and temperature within the experimental vial led to cracking of the lid and some evaporation of water. In these cases, the δ 18 O water value of the end water was higher than that of the start water by a maximum of~5 ‰ and the value used in subsequent calculations is the mean of the two valuesi.e. assuming a linear progression in both evaporative enrichment of 18 O in water and the degree of silica-water isotopic exchange.
steps.searchable_summary: If evaporation occurred (end water δ18O > start water δ18O), calculate mean δ18O value of start and end water for subsequent calculations

steps.description: The oxygen isotopic composition of waters was analysed using the equilibration method 38 with a Sira 10 dual-inlet isotope ratio mass spectrometer...
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze water oxygen isotopic composition using equilibration method and dual-inlet isotope ratio mass spectrometer

methods.description: Determining the oxygen isotope ratio of cleaned biogenic silica samples using stepwise fluorination with BrF5, conversion of liberated oxygen to CO2, and measurement via isotope ratio mass spectrometry.

steps.description: For determination of the oxygen isotope ratio of biogenic silica, the hydrous outer layer of the frustules was removed by stepwise fluorination whereby a stoichiometric deficiency of the reagent, bromine pentafluoride (BrF 5 ), was used to partially react the samples...
steps.searchable_summary: Remove hydrous outer layer by stepwise fluorination using deficient BrF5

steps.description: ...before full reaction at 500°C with an excess of BrF 5 .
steps.searchable_summary: Perform full reaction at 500°C with excess BrF5 to liberate oxygen

steps.description: The oxygen liberated was converted into CO 2 by passing over hot graphite, following the method of Clayton and Mayeda. 39
steps.searchable_summary: Convert liberated oxygen to CO2 by passing over hot graphite

steps.description: Oxygen yields were monitored for comparison with the calculated theoretical yield.
steps.searchable_summary: Monitor oxygen yields and compare to theoretical yield

steps.description: A random selection of samples was analysed in duplicate and gave a mean reproducibility of 0.3 % (1σ), which is comparable with the 0.3% reproducibility of the standard laboratory diatomite control (BFC mod ) both within individual batches and over the longer term. 22
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze random samples in duplicate and compare reproducibility (vs. standard)

steps.description: The 18 O/ 16 O ratios were measured on a MAT 253 isotope ratio mass spectrometer (ThermoFinnigan, Bremen, Germany)...
steps.searchable_summary: Measure 18O/16O ratios using isotope ratio mass spectrometer

steps.description: ...and normalised through laboratory standards and NBS28. 22
steps.searchable_summary: Normalize measured ratios using laboratory standards and NBS28

steps.description: The data are reported in the usual δ form, as per mil (‰) deviations from VSMOW.
steps.searchable_summary: Report data as δ18O values (per mil vs. VSMOW)
